course_info.title: Digital Marketing and Advertising
prerequisites: DC201 - Social Media Strategy and Management

learning_objectives: Map the digital marketing landscape including search, social, email, display, and affiliate channels
Explain the customer journey and how different channels serve different stages
Distinguish between inbound and outbound digital marketing strategies
readings: Google, 'Think with Google' - Customer Journey Mapping tools

lecture_notes.introduction: Digital marketing and advertising is where communication strategy meets measurable business outcomes. This course bridges the creative communication skills from your earlier courses with the analytical and strategic skills needed to drive traffic, generate leads, and convert customers through digital channels.
key_concepts: Digital marketing channels: search (SEO/SEM), social media, email, content marketing, display, affiliate, influencer
The marketing funnel: awareness, consideration, conversion, retention, advocacy
Inbound vs. outbound: attracting through value (content, SEO) vs. interrupting with ads (display, paid search)
Customer journey mapping: identifying touchpoints and influence at each decision stage
Integrated digital marketing: coordinating channels for compounding effect rather than operating in silos
lecture_notes.demonstration: Tracing a real customer journey from first Google search through social media engagement, email nurture, and final purchase, identifying each touchpoint and channel's role in the conversion
setup_requirements: Customer journey mapping template (provided on LMS)

review.value: A strategy that attracts customers through valuable content, SEO, and social media rather than interruptive advertising, building trust and authority that naturally draws prospects into the sales funnel
The complete path a person takes from first awareness of a brand through consideration, purchase, and post-purchase experience, with each stage requiring different communication approaches

class_schedule.topic: Search Engine Marketing: SEO and Paid Search
learning_objectives: Differentiate between organic search (SEO) and paid search (SEM/PPC) strategies
Conduct keyword research and competitive analysis for a search campaign
Calculate quality score factors and their impact on ad placement and cost
readings: Enge et al., The Art of SEO, Ch. 1-3
Google Ads, 'Getting Started with Search Campaigns'

lecture_notes.introduction: Search is the highest-intent digital channel because people are actively looking for something. Mastering both organic and paid search gives communicators access to audiences at the moment they are most receptive to your message.
key_concepts: SEO fundamentals: on-page optimization, technical SEO, content quality, backlink authority
Paid search (PPC): bidding on keywords to appear in search results above organic listings
Quality Score: Google's rating of ad relevance based on expected CTR, ad relevance, and landing page experience
Keyword match types: broad, phrase, exact, and negative keywords
Search intent alignment: matching ad copy and landing pages to the searcher's actual need
lecture_notes.demonstration: Running a keyword research session for a local business using Google Keyword Planner, identifying target keywords, analyzing competitor ads, and drafting ad copy for three ad groups
setup_requirements: Google Keyword Planner access (via Google Ads account)
Google Ads demo account or sandbox

review.value: Google's 1-10 rating of ad quality based on expected click-through rate, ad relevance to the keyword, and landing page experience; higher Quality Scores lower cost per click and improve ad position
Keywords you explicitly exclude from triggering your ads, preventing wasted spend on irrelevant searches; e.g., a luxury hotel adding 'cheap' as a negative keyword

learning_objectives: Explain how programmatic advertising and real-time bidding work
Design effective display ad creative following best practices
Set up audience targeting and retargeting for display campaigns
readings: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB), 'Display Ad Standards and Specifications'

lecture_notes.introduction: Display advertising reaches people across the web, not just when they are searching. Understanding how programmatic buying works and how to create effective display creative allows you to build awareness and retarget prospects throughout their browsing experience.
key_concepts: Programmatic advertising: automated ad buying using algorithms and real-time bidding
Real-time bidding (RTB): the auction process where ad impressions are bought and sold in milliseconds
Display ad formats: standard banners, rich media, native ads, video pre-roll
Audience targeting: demographic, behavioral, contextual, and retargeting approaches
Retargeting: serving ads to people who previously visited your site or engaged with your content
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up a retargeting campaign in Google Display Network: defining the audience pixel, selecting placements, uploading creative in standard IAB sizes, and configuring frequency capping
setup_requirements: IAB standard ad size reference sheet

review.value: An automated auction system where ad impressions are bought and sold in milliseconds as a web page loads, with the winning bidder's ad displayed to the user based on targeting parameters and bid price
Limiting the number of times a single user sees the same ad within a time period, preventing ad fatigue and wasted impressions while maintaining positive brand perception

learning_objectives: Design landing pages that convert traffic into leads or sales
Apply A/B testing methodology to optimize conversion rates
Identify and fix common conversion killers on existing landing pages
readings: Unbounce, 'The Ultimate Guide to Landing Pages'

lecture_notes.introduction: Driving traffic is pointless if your landing page does not convert. A well-optimized landing page can double or triple your conversion rate with the same traffic, making it the highest-leverage optimization in digital marketing.
key_concepts: Landing page anatomy: headline, value proposition, supporting evidence, CTA, trust signals
Message match: ensuring the landing page delivers on the promise made in the ad or link
Friction reduction: minimizing form fields, eliminating navigation distractions, simplifying the path
A/B testing: isolating one variable at a time, statistical significance, sample size requirements
Conversion rate optimization (CRO): systematic testing and improvement of conversion elements
lecture_notes.demonstration: Analyzing three real landing pages using a conversion audit checklist, identifying specific issues, and proposing A/B tests for each, with predicted impact estimates
setup_requirements: Landing page audit checklist (provided on LMS)

review.value: The alignment between what an ad or link promises and what the landing page delivers; when the headline, visuals, and offer on the landing page mirror the ad, conversion rates increase significantly
The confidence level (typically 95%) that an observed difference in A/B test results is real rather than due to random chance, requiring sufficient sample size and test duration

learning_objectives: Configure goal tracking in Google Analytics to measure conversions
Compare attribution models and explain when each is appropriate
Build a marketing dashboard that connects channel performance to business outcomes
readings: Google, 'Attribution Overview' in Google Analytics documentation

lecture_notes.introduction: The ability to measure what works and what does not is what makes digital marketing fundamentally different from traditional advertising. Marketing analytics transforms spending decisions from guesswork into data-driven optimization.
key_concepts: Goal tracking: defining conversions (purchases, sign-ups, downloads) and configuring tracking in analytics platforms
UTM parameters: tagging campaign URLs to track traffic source, medium, campaign, content, and term
Attribution models: last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, position-based, and data-driven
Marketing dashboards: visualizing KPIs in real-time for decision-making
ROI calculation: revenue generated minus marketing cost, divided by marketing cost
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a Google Analytics goal funnel for an e-commerce checkout, adding UTM parameters to campaign links, and comparing how different attribution models credit the same conversion path
setup_requirements: UTM builder tool (Google Campaign URL Builder)
Dashboard template (Google Data Studio or equivalent)

review.value: Tags added to URLs (source, medium, campaign, content, term) that allow analytics platforms to identify which specific marketing efforts drove traffic, enabling precise channel and campaign attribution

Frameworks for assigning conversion credit to touchpoints: last-click (all credit to final interaction), first-click (all credit to discovery), linear (equal credit to all), time-decay (more credit to recent), and data-driven (machine learning allocation)

learning_objectives: Allocate a marketing budget across channels based on objectives and expected ROI
Create a media plan with reach, frequency, and cost projections
Adjust budget allocation based on campaign performance data

lecture_notes.introduction: Strategy without budget is a wish list. Learning to allocate limited resources across channels, project outcomes, and optimize spending based on performance data is the skill that earns digital marketers a seat at the business strategy table.
key_concepts: Budget allocation frameworks: percentage of revenue, objective-based, competitive parity, test-and-scale
Channel cost benchmarks: average CPM, CPC, and CPA by channel and industry
Media plan components: channels, targeting, creative, budget, timeline, KPIs, and contingency
Performance-based reallocation: shifting budget from underperforming to overperforming channels
Testing budgets: allocating 10-20% for experimentation with new channels and approaches
lecture_notes.demonstration: Creating a $5,000/month media plan for a B2B SaaS company: allocating across Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, and content promotion, projecting reach and leads, and building a reallocation decision framework
setup_requirements: Media planning spreadsheet template (provided on LMS)

review.value: Allocating 10-20% of the total budget to test new channels, audiences, or creative approaches, then scaling investment in the approaches that outperform benchmarks and cutting those that underperform
Total campaign cost divided by the number of conversions (acquisitions), representing the actual cost to acquire one customer or lead through a specific channel or campaign

questions.question: Explain what Google Ads Quality Score measures and why a high Quality Score benefits the advertiser financially.
questions.answer: Quality Score is Google's 1-10 rating of ad quality based on three factors: expected click-through rate (how likely users are to click), ad relevance (how closely the ad matches the keyword's intent), and landing page experience (how relevant and useful the landing page is). A high Quality Score benefits the advertiser financially because Google rewards higher-quality ads with lower cost-per-click and better ad positions. An advertiser with a Quality Score of 8 can pay less per click than a competitor with a Quality Score of 4 and still appear higher in search results, because Google prioritizes user experience and wants to show the most relevant ads.

rubric.key: Lists all three Quality Score components

questions.question: Define retargeting and explain why it typically has a higher conversion rate than prospecting campaigns.
questions.answer: Retargeting is the practice of serving ads to people who have previously visited your website, engaged with your content, or interacted with your brand in some measurable way. It typically has a higher conversion rate than prospecting campaigns because the audience has already demonstrated interest by taking a previous action. They are further down the marketing funnel than cold prospects. Retargeting keeps the brand present during the consideration phase and can address specific abandonment points (e.g., cart abandonment ads showing the exact products left behind).

rubric.key: Explains higher conversion with prior interest reasoning

questions.question: What is message match in landing page optimization, and describe a scenario where poor message match would hurt conversion rates.
questions.answer: Message match is the alignment between what an ad or link promises and what the landing page delivers in terms of headline, visuals, and offer. Poor message match example: a Google Ad with the headline 'Free 30-Day Trial of Project Management Software' that sends users to the company's homepage rather than a dedicated trial signup page. The visitor clicked expecting to start a free trial immediately but instead lands on a generic page about the company, requiring navigation to find the trial offer. This disconnect between expectation and experience causes visitors to leave rather than hunt for the original promise.

questions.question: Compare last-click and linear attribution models. A user discovers a brand through a blog post, returns via an email newsletter, and converts after clicking a retargeting ad. How would each model assign credit?
questions.answer: Last-click attribution gives 100% of the conversion credit to the final touchpoint before conversion. In this scenario, the retargeting ad receives all the credit, and the blog post and email newsletter receive none. Linear attribution divides credit equally among all touchpoints. In this scenario, the blog post, email newsletter, and retargeting ad each receive 33.3% of the credit. Last-click undervalues awareness and nurturing channels, while linear treats all touchpoints as equally important regardless of their role in the journey. Neither is perfect, which is why data-driven attribution models attempt to weight touchpoints by their actual influence.

rubric.key: Correctly applies last-click to the scenario
Correctly applies linear to the scenario
Evaluates strengths and weaknesses of each

major_assignments.title: Integrated Digital Marketing Campaign
major_assignments.description: Develop a complete integrated digital marketing campaign for a real or fictional business. The campaign must span at least three channels, include ad creative, landing page wireframes, a media plan with budget allocation, and a measurement framework with KPIs and attribution model.
objectives: Apply customer journey mapping to identify optimal channel mix
Create search, social, and display ad creative with proper targeting
Design conversion-optimized landing pages for campaign traffic
Build a measurement framework connecting channel metrics to business outcomes
deliverables: Campaign brief (2-3 pages) defining objectives, audience, channels, and success metrics
Ad creative package: 3 search ads, 3 social ads, and 2 display ads with targeting specifications
Landing page wireframe with annotated conversion elements
Media plan spreadsheet with budget allocation, reach projections, and timeline
Measurement framework defining KPIs, attribution model, and reporting schedule

criteria: Campaign objectives are SMART and aligned to business goals
Customer journey mapping informs channel selection
Audience targeting is well-defined and justified
Budget allocation is logical and supported by benchmark data
Ad creative follows platform specifications and best practices
Message match between ads and landing page is strong
Landing page wireframe includes all essential conversion elements
Creative variations enable meaningful A/B testing
KPIs are defined for each channel and funnel stage
Attribution model is selected and justified for the campaign type
UTM parameters and tracking plan are documented
Strategic campaign demonstrating customer journey understanding
Professional ad creative and landing page design
Rigorous measurement framework with appropriate attribution model
Complete media plan with justified budget allocation

quizzes.description: Regular quizzes on digital marketing concepts and channel strategies

platform_exercises.description: Hands-on exercises in Google Ads, Meta Ads, and analytics platforms

major_assignment.description: Integrated digital marketing campaign with creative, media plan, and measurement

midterm_examination.description: Written and practical examination on search, social, and display marketing

final_examination.description: Cumulative examination including campaign analysis and budget optimization

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all sessions. Platform exercises are conducted in class and cannot be replicated independently. More than 3 absences result in a half-letter grade reduction.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late assignments lose 10% per day. Campaign deliverables with missed deadlines cannot be accepted after 5 days since feedback cycles depend on timely submission.
course_policies.collaboration: Platform exercises may be completed in pairs. All graded assignments are individual work. Sharing campaign strategies, creative assets, or analytics configurations between students is prohibited.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All data used in reports must be accurate and from stated sources. Fabricating analytics data or campaign results is academic fraud. Ad creative must be original or use properly licensed assets.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Students need a Google Ads account (free setup with no spending required for exercises), Google Analytics access, Meta Ads Manager access, and a laptop with internet access. Ad platform sandbox environments are provided where available.
